action.hypothesis: `routed` at the refreshed low-K mainline target (`K=32`, `EF=12`) remained weak, so the next informative move is to keep that target fixed and test a different non-TopK architecture family with real prior signal. Run `two_stage_residual` at `K=32`, `EF=12` with the shared `signum`/`LR=1.6e-3`/`AUXK_ALPHA=0` recipe to see whether coarse-to-fine residual allocation recovers useful low-K quality where explicit routing did not.
action.summary: Hold the refreshed low-K comparison point at `K=32`, `EF=12` and switch only architecture from `routed` to `two_stage_residual`. This continues the stagnation-breaking architecture search while preserving the lower-K focus.
action.self_review: This keeps the round architecture-led under severe stagnation and preserves the lower-K, same-EF target introduced last round. The main risk is that `two_stage_residual` was previously weak at smaller EF, but its prior full-signal evidence makes it a better next non-TopK comparison than reopening archived TopK-style families.
notes_to_memory: Round 72 keeps the refreshed low-K mainline target fixed at `K=32`, `EF=12` and changes only architecture after `routed` remained weak.
`two_stage_residual` is the next non-TopK comparison because it has prior real full-signal evidence and tests hierarchical residual allocation rather than support routing or TopK variants.
next_hypotheses: If `two_stage_residual` at `K=32`, `EF=12` is competitive, keep the family fixed and map `K=40` before touching EF.
If `two_stage_residual` at `K=32`, `EF=12` is still weak, pivot to another fundamentally different non-TopK direction in the same mainline band rather than reopening archived TopK-family comparisons.
